content.problemStatement:
Chef has started learning to solve dynamic programming problems recently. He has practiced on a lot of problems already, so he wants to try his hands on the hardest problems in this domain.

Chef only likes the interesting part of solving dynamic programming problems ‚ÄĒ specifically, he just likes to divide a problem into beautiful subproblems which consist of solving recurrences; he finds actually solving these recurrences quite boring. Since you are Chef's assistant, your job is solving this boring part for Chef.

Let's define a recurrent sequence {dp(k)}k=1‚ąě\left\lbrace{dp(k)}\right\rbrace_{k=1}^\infty{dp(k)}k=1‚ąě‚Äč with parametres AAA, BBB and KKK as follows:


dp(1)=Kdp(1) = Kdp(1)=K
for n>1n \gt 1n>1, dp(n)=A‚čÖdp(n‚ąí1)+B‚čÖ‚ąĎi=1n‚ąí1dp(i)‚čÖdp(n‚ąíi)dp(n) = A \cdot dp(n-1) + B\cdot \sum\limits_{i=1}^{n-1} dp(i) \cdot dp(n-i)dp(n)=A‚čÖdp(n‚ąí1)+B‚čÖi=1‚ąĎn‚ąí1‚Äčdp(i)‚čÖdp(n‚ąíi)
Chef would like you to find the sum ‚ąĎi=LRdp(i)2\sum\limits_{i=L}^{R} dp(i)^2i=L‚ąĎR‚Äčdp(i)2. Since this number can be very large, compute it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7. He wants to do this for QQQ pairs of LLL and RRR.
constraints: ExplanationThe first four terms of this sequence are:

dp(4)=2‚čÖdp(3)+dp(1)‚čÖdp(3)+dp(2)‚čÖdp(2)+dp(3)‚čÖdp(1)=57dp(4) = 2 \cdot dp(3) + dp(1) \cdot dp(3) + dp(2) \cdot dp(2) + dp(3) \cdot dp(1) = 57dp(4)=2‚čÖdp(3)+dp(1)‚čÖdp(3)+dp(2)‚čÖdp(2)+dp(3)‚čÖdp(1)=57
The answers for first and second query are:
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains four space-separated integers NNN, KKK, AAA and BBB.
The second line of the input contains QQQ.
The next QQQ lines contains two space-separated integers LLL and RRR.
content.outputFormat: For every query print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the sum ‚ąĎi=LRdp(i)2\sum\limits_{i=L}^{R} dp(i)^2i=L‚ąĎR‚Äčdp(i)2 modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
